title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following the discovery of the body in the sarcophagus, an unidentified man expresses distress, comparing the deceased woman to a piece of art from Naples. As Detective Bacon and Campbell walk toward the manor, two boys on bicycles, Alexander Eastrie and James Stottard West, plead to see the body. Bacon allows them to enter the warehouse through an officer named Sanders.

Bacon summons Lucy Esperanza, the Crickenshaw family's maid, for questioning. Lucy explains that she went to the warehouse to find paint, but she actually used a crowbar to open the sarcophagus because she was searching for a body. She reveals she was hired for this task by Jane Marple, an elderly woman residing at 4 Madison Road. Lucy expresses fear that if Bacon informs her employer, Emma, about her true motives, she will lose her job and her ability to support her family.

Bacon discusses the case with the county police chief, noting that Lucy is likely not a local. They plan to hold an inquest the following day, hoping members of the Crickenshaw family—including Harold, Alfred, and the painter Sederick—might identify the deceased. Bacon expresses skepticism regarding Jane Marple’s "fantastical" claims but acknowledges that she successfully hired Lucy to find the body.

The inquest concludes that the cause of death was strangulation, though no suspects are identified. Following the proceedings, members of the Crickenshaw family, including Emma, Cecil, Harold, Brian Eastleigh, and the lawyer Mr. Wimborne, depart in a limousine. Alexander and James West, who had watched the inquest from the hall, approach the departing group.
